mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically increasing present valuations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 7.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth expectations, boosting corporate profit margins and triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns within the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 7.0 percent over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and consumer credit outstanding signal synchronized real-economy demand expansion. This joint signal compresses credit spreads and lifts equity valuations over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term (HOUST and RSAFS z-scores) shows a negative or statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression predicting 20-day SPX returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery periods, rising industrial production indicates capacity utilization normalization. This supply-side expansion improves earnings guidance and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns turn negative when INDPRO rises for three consecutive months during identified post-shock recovery windows, contradicting the supply-expansion premium.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent confirms robust aggregate demand, which historically correlates with elevated corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate exceeds 2.5 percent
failure_condition: Rank IC between quarterly GDPC1 growth and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.02 across all non-overlapping 12-month windows in the sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls directly feeds into near-term earnings revisions. Positive payroll surprises consistently drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The median 5-day forward return following positive monthly PAYEMS changes is not statistically different from zero (p > 0.05) over a 10-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rises in the consumer price index and the federal funds rate indicate active monetary tightening against persistent inflation. This combination raises real discount rates and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative 60-day SPX returns in more than 40% of historical instances, or the interaction coefficient becomes positive in a rolling 3-year regression.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansion environments, decelerating housing starts combined with plateauing employment growth signal demand exhaustion. This regime shift triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings revisions turn downward.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines for two consecutive months while PAYEMS growth falls below 150,000
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of late-cycle instances where HOUST and PAYEMS exhibit the specified deceleration pattern, invalidating the demand-exhaustion premise.
